candidate_pkrs.outcome: Inject lifecycle timing, add graceful-close then SIGKILL bounds, fail closed after the second bound, and prove abort, cleanup, stubborn child, and double close
Bound ACP connection close and child exit, escalate to SIGKILL, fail closed after the second bound, and prove stubborn transport, stubborn child, cleared maps, and double close

Add explicit Codex ACP auth configuration and close authority-changing managed arguments; replace Claude free-form extraArgs with a non-authority allowlist or remove it without treating explicit grant as undeclared
Add test-only public-scope graph trace and provider-neutral double-close cases for both module namespaces, with no runtime facade or shared scope factory

payload.decision_target: Close the remaining AG-2 lifecycle and AG-3 authority uncertainty without broadening the shared runtime contract
questions_answered: AG-2 requires two bounded phases: graceful protocol close or SIGINT/SIGTERM until bound one, then SIGKILL until bound two. If exit or transport closure still does not settle, cleanup must reject, report a nonzero live-resource read, and open breaking; it must never wait forever or claim zero.

Ambient environment is AG-3 only where it supplies undeclared credentials, roots, or permissions. Claude auth global explicitly selects the ambient credential chain; Claude token explicitly names its credential environment. Codex ACP has a real gap because CodexAcpConfig has no auth choice even though its child inherits credentials.

A configured command and benign protocol arguments are explicit engine configuration, not automatically AG-3. Only arguments that can override credentials, roots, permissions, tools, MCP, or session identity activate AG-3. Blanket rejection of all command or args configuration would be overreach.

Claude extraArgs is a real future-drift AG-3 risk because a denylist can miss a new authority flag. The managed surface should remove free-form extraArgs or replace it with a narrow allowlist that cannot alter named authority.

Codex permission grant is coarse but explicit, so it is not an undeclared grant under the confirmed AG-3 metric. Deny remains required as the safe policy in examples; removing explicit grant needs a separate human policy decision.

The minimum static graph proof is a public createScope test that traces model.complete to provider turn to declared run, plus a run preset proving no engine starts. The minimum lifecycle idempotence proof calls context close and scope dispose twice and asserts one termination, cleared correlation state, closed transport, and zero live child.
Stubborn-child tests must separately prove graceful timeout, SIGKILL escalation, second-bound rejection, and no false zero-live-resource claim.
questions_unanswered: Human policy has not removed Codex explicit grant mode
Exact bound values need an orchestrator choice or provider config defaults accepted as part of the PKR review
Independent validation has not accepted this checkpoint
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: AG-2 remains blocked until both providers converge or fail closed within two bounds
AG-3 should target credential declaration and authority-changing arguments, not all environment or command configuration
No shared ManagedSession, MCP, or tool surface is needed
